_purpose: Hand-curated per-chapter paid roll structure and metadata. Entries are keyed by the mechanical/predicted-roll chapter. When a chapter has an entry here, it FULLY determines that chapter's paid roll list — grouping (multi-grab) AND order. Each entry in 'rolls' is one roll object: {perks: [name, ...], outcome: 'hit'|'miss', constellation, word_position, mention_chapter_num, mention_word_position, display_position_policy, evidence_quotes}. mention_chapter_num is where the author narrates/lists the result and defaults to the mechanical chapter. display_position_policy is 'mention', 'mechanical', 'section_start', 'source_marker', or 'section_end'. Without an entry, default behaviour: each paid obtained_perks row is its own roll, in obtained_perks order.

evidence_quotes.text: Two of the motes were secured (PR multi-grab).
Workshop: Electronics is a separate Toolkits roll.
Two motes with another smaller one attached and another unrelated mote — four independent connections.
Then The Pond.
Then Pilot Simulator.
Secured two connections (PR multi-grab).
Then Nano-Forge.

I could feel my passenger's excitement as my reach grew for the final grasp. A mote swung close from one of the mid-sized constellations. My power tried to grasp it, but the energy was too much. The flare of energy spun away and out of reach.

I felt myself latch on to a mote of power. It was from one of the smaller constellations and a little less powerful than the mote that I had missed on my previous attempt. As my connection solidified I felt my knowledge of the power and its constellation expand.

This was not a tinker power. This was something better. This was a brute power that required tinker support.

That constellation was called 'Clothing'. I didn't have a good sense of the other powers within it, but this power was called 'Fashion'.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups the Star Trek Skills acquisitions into one Knowledge roll.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table records this as a Toolkits miss.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups Minor Blessing Demeter - Cultivation and Unnatural Skill: Naturecraft as one 300 CP Quality roll.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table records the eight Entryway/Entrance Hall acquisitions as a single x8 roll.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table records Firestorm as a Resources and Durability hit.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups Minor Blessing Apollo - Medicine and Unnatural Skill: Healing as one Quality roll.

Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups Basic Nutrition, A Little Less Basic, and A Lot Less as one 500 CP roll. The source note says some late rows may say Clothing even though Clothing had been replaced by Personal Reality after its perks were exhausted.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups the Percy Jackson Minor Blessing and Unnatural Skill acquisitions as one Quality roll.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups the Library collection upgrades as one 200 CP Personal Reality roll.
Author-vetted 2026-05-09 roll table groups Music Collection and Movie & TV Series Collection as one 100 CP Personal Reality roll.
I felt my power try and fail to latch onto a mote from a new constellation.

One of the largest constellations was swinging towards me and my power latched onto a small mote from it. I took a moment to focus on the new power. The constellation was 'Quality' and this particular power was called 'Bling of War'.

It was the same constellation I had failed to connect to with my first attempt. This time my power was strong enough to latch onto a mote as they flew past. Unlike last time this took every ounce of my stored energy to secure the link. Finally I had the connection and could tell what that constellation was.

It was the 'Alchemy' constellation and the mote I had connected to was 'Evermore Alchemist'.
My power latched on to one of the smaller motes and secured a connection. The constellation was 'Vehicles' and the mote was called 'Black Thumb'.
During the fight with Oni Lee my power had failed to latch onto one of the larger motes from the Vehicle constellation.
This time it connected with a small mote from what turned out to be the 'Time' constellation.

That was when I felt a constellation approach within the Celestial Forge. I still had extra reach from my last connection and it had grown further since then. A link solidified to a small mote in the 'Knowledge' constellation.

The ability I latched onto was called 'Not a Stupid Grunt'.
the approach of the Knowledge constellation within the Celestial Forge. My power failed to latch onto the nearest mote

The Celestial Forge moved and focused on one of the arrays of powers. The Toolkits constellation was swinging towards me and my power latched onto a small mote, securing the connection. It was called Laboratorium.

The next morning I woke up to the feeling of the Knowledge constellation in the Celestial Forge swinging by me. My power's reach was the strongest it had ever been and was just enough to latch on to a mid-sized mote. The ability was called 'Grease Monkey'.
While I was waiting my power completely failed to grab a mote from one of the constellations I had yet to identify.

model_validation_resolution.note: Chapter 4 appears to have been edited after the original roll schedule; all five narrated attempts are legitimate and the fifth is anchored by its direct miss evidence.

evidence_quotes.text: A new constellation approached and my power was latching onto a mote from it. It turned out to be the 'Crafting' constellation and the mote was 'Smithing'.
The Vehicles constellation swung towards me and my power latched onto a mote from it.
a new constellation spun past in the Celestial Forge without my power linking to it.
My power managed to link to a mote from a new constellation. The 'Magic' constellation.
This particular ability was called 'Enchanting'.
While I worked my power failed to connect to another mote from the magic constellation. It spun off and my reach continued to build.
another constellation swung close in the Celestial Forge. This one was called 'Size'
The ability was called 'Nanite Sciences'

model_validation_resolution.note: Curator confirmed chapter 6.1 has 1 known attempts despite 0 predicted model slots.

evidence_quotes.text: I felt the Celestial Forge moving again, with my power connecting onto another mote.
My reach was bigger than before and had enough power to latch onto a mid-sized mote from a new constellation. This one was called "Resources and Durability' and the power was called 'Built to Last'.
On the way home my power failed to make a connection to one of the largest motes of the Vehicles constellation
I felt my power move again and connect to a small mote from the Quality constellation. This one was called Decadence
Eventually a failed link to the Vehicle constellation in the Celestial Forge drove me completely awake.

model_validation_resolution.note: Curator confirmed chapter 7 has 4 known attempts despite 3 predicted model slots.

evidence_quotes.text: My power activated again as I felt the Forge move. The Time constellation was approaching again and my power connected to a mote called 'Machinist'.
My power moved again and made another connection to the Resources and Durability constellation. The ability was called 'Rationing'.
a few failed connections
a few failed connections
my power had latched on to the strongest mote it had managed so far. It was from the Clothing constellation
Life Fibers
my forge had missed a couple of attempted connections
my forge had missed a couple of attempted connections
the Clothing constellation swung by and made a link
It was the Alchemy constellation and my power latched to a tiny mote. The oddly named ability was called Kazooie Alchemy.
The Size constellation passed by without any successful connections.
On my way back to the workshop I felt the Celestial Forge move and the last constellation I had yet to identify came into reach. My power was able to link to the smallest of the motes
The final constellation was called Magitech.
The new ability I received was called Mechanist.
This time it connected to a small mote from the Resources and Durability constellation. It was called Repair Savvy.
During lunch my power missed a connection from the alchemy constellation and my reach continued to grow.
I felt the forge again. This time it was the Quality constellation and I connected to a small mote called Stylish Mechanic.
I felt my power fail to make a connection to the Alchemy constellation and ignored it, staying focused on the call.

model_validation_resolution.note: Curator confirmed chapter 10 has 5 known attempts despite 4 predicted model slots.

evidence_quotes.text: I felt the Celestial Forge move again, bringing the Alchemy constellation close by. This time I was able to connect to a mid-sized mote. The ability was called Deranged Alchemist.
The Celestial Forge moved again and my power failed to connect to a mote from the Quality Constellation.
My power failed to connect to a mote from the Vehicles constellation
I felt the Celestial Forge move again, this time bringing the Size constellation within reach.
The mote was called Master Builder
My power missed a connection to the Clothing constellation
ignored the Celestial Forge as it missed a connection to the Size constellation.
the vehicle constellation swung towards me and my power connected to a small mote called Mechanic.
the Forge moved again and missed a connection in the Quality constellation.
I felt the Celestial Forge again. It was another mote from the Size constellation
The mote was called Nanite Removal and Control.
The Celestial Forge missed a connection to the Magic constellation
The Celestial Forge missed a connection to the Time constellation

all the reach I had built was used to connect to a larger mote from the Size constellation

The Celestial Forge missed a connection to the Clothing constellation
I felt the Celestial Forge pull the Vehicle constellation towards me and connect to one of the smaller motes called Fingers of Silver.
I felt the Celestial Forge again, this time bringing the Knowledge constellation within reach.
The entire cluster was called Skills and the one I was able to link to was Physics.
The forge missed another connection to the Knowledge constellation
the Celestial Forge circled around again, once again bringing the Size constellation into reach
My power connected to a similar mote to my previous one, but this was only two motes clustered rather than twelve. I had enough reach to secure connections with both of them
The motes were called Science! Mechanics and Science! Engineering
As I watched the approaching constellation most of my concerns vanished. Toolkits.

Fortunately I managed to connect with something. Unfortunately it was the smallest mote in the constellation, possibly the smallest in the entire Celestial Forge. It was actually two motes, not related in any way, just bunched up to look like a normal mote. The toolkit constellation had finally given me some tools.
The first mote was called Diagnostic Tools.
The second item was called Micromanipulators.
The Celestial Forge missed a connection with the Magic constellation

it was called Class: Engineer, Specialization: Mechanic

As we moved out I felt the Celestial Forge make a connection to a mid-sized note from the Vehicles constellation. It was another cluster, this one with four equally sized motes in it. The power was called Valuable Memories and was something of a conundrum. I could tell the first mote was about vehicles, the third mote was seriously about vehicles. The fourth motes was about something at least on the scale of vehicles. I connected to the second mote. Do you want to know what the second mote was about?
The nature of memories.
the Celestial Forge made another connection to the Crafting constellation. This was a smaller mote called Fingers of the North Star.
the Celestial Forge missed a connection to the Size constellation
the Celestial Forge missed a connection to the Size constellation

the Celestial Forge connected to the Crafting constellation
